qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Verification-email resend endpoint exposes account existence and email-verification state
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The advisory is a Parse Server Cloud Function prototype-chain resolution flaw in Cloud Code dispatch that can cause infinite recursion DoS or dispatch bypass. This finding is an account-enumeration oracle in the verification-email resend flow, with a different endpoint, code path, and root cause.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No final qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described Cloud Function prototype-chain dispatch root cause. The session's only qualifying finding is a separate verification-email account-enumeration issue.
evidence: The advisory describes Parse Server DoS and Cloud Function dispatch bypass via prototype-chain resolution on Cloud Function endpoint names such as Object.prototype properties and dot-notation traversal.
The run manifest lists exactly one final qualifying finding: /Users/lumi/ziyue/EvoHunt-Run/advisory_ccbb16e2/verification_email_account_enumeration.
That qualifying finding targets POST /verificationEmailRequest account-existence and verification-state disclosure, not Cloud Code dispatch.

The advisory-relevant Cloud Code family was retained only as a no_vuln result in cloud_code_dispatch_request_denylist_object_shape/vulnerability_report.json, which states prototype-chain Cloud Function dispatch is blocked by null-prototype trigger stores and no shipped default-path vulnerability remained in that family.
